input.prompt:
After upgrading to MSTest 4.1.0, my test project fails to compile. I have a sealed
custom TestMethodAttribute subclass that overrides Execute, and several tests use
[Timeout(TestTimeout.Infinite)]. How do I fix these errors?

data.content:

custom TestMethodAttribute subclass that overrides Execute, and several tests use
[Timeout(TestTimeout.Infinite)]. How do I fix these errors?

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Find MSTest 4.1.0 assembly and XML docs

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Find MSTest 4.1.0 assembly and XML docs

data.reasoningText:
Two issues in MSTest 4.1.0:

1. **CS0115**: `TestMethodAttribute.Execute(ITestMethod)` is no longer virtual/overridable. In MSTest v3, you could override `Execute`, but in MSTest 4.x, the extensibility model changed. The class is `sealed` too, which is fine for the attribute itself but the base method is no longer overridable.

2. **CS0103**: `TestTimeout.Infinite` was removed. The replacement is `Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan` or just removing the timeout, or using `0` to mean infinite.
   
Looking at the MSTest 4.x migration guide, the `Execute` method on `TestMethodAttribute` was removed entirely as part of a redesigned extensibility model. Since `TimedTestMethodAttribute` is sealed and inherits from `TestMethodAttribute`, it can't override a method that no longer exists on the base class.

For the timeout issue, `TestTimeout` was removed in MSTest 4.x, and the `[Timeout]` attribute now expects milliseconds directly as a parameter rather than using a separate timeout class.

Let me examine the actual MSTest 4.1.0 API to determine the correct migration path.

arguments.description: Find MSTest 4.1.0 assembly and XML docs

toolCalls.args: {"command": "find ~/.nuget/packages/mstest.testframework/4.1.0 -name \"*.xml\" | head -5 && find ~/.nuget/packages/mstest.testframework/4.1.0 -name \"Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestPlatform.TestFramework.dll\" | head -3", "description": "Find MSTest 4.1.0 assembly and XML docs", "initial_wait": 10}

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-eaea91d36b584d68b9def818eb12b799 && dotnet tool install --global dotnet-ildasm 2>/dev/null; dotnet-ildasm ~/.nuget/packages/mstest.testframework/4.1.0/lib/net8.0/Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestPlatform.TestFramework.dll 2>/dev/null | grep -A5 "TestMethodAttribute" | head -30 || python3 -c "
import subprocess, re
# Use dotnet to decompile and check
result = subprocess.run(['dotnet', 'tool', 'list', '-g'], capture_output=True, text=True)
print(result.stdout)
"

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
<member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute">
            This attribute doesn't currently provide any different functionality compared to <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/>. It's only
            present for backward compatibility. Using <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/> is recommended, even for parameterized tests.
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)">
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.DataTestMethodAttribute"/> class.
            Multiple methods with this attribute in the assembly is allowed, but there is no guarantee of the order in which they will be executed.
            The test cleanup attribute marks methods that are executed after every test marked with a <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute"/>.
            Each thread of execution will be handed a TestClass worth of tests to execute.
            Within the TestClass, the test methods will execute serially.
            Each thread of execution will be handed TestMethods to execute.
            Multiple methods with this attribute in the assembly is allowed, but there is no guarantee of the order in which they will be executed. In addition, TimeoutAttribute isn't supported on methods with this attribute.
            Note that before RetryAttribute is considered, the test was already executed once.
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryAttribute.ExecuteAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryContext)">
            Note that a first run of the method was already executed and failed before this method is called.
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryBaseAttribute.ExecuteAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryContext)">
            Note that a first run of the method was already executed and failed before this method is called.
        <member name="P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.RetryContext.ExecuteTaskGetter">
            Gets the function that will execute the test asynchronously.
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestClassAttribute.GetTestMethodAttribute(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute)">
        <member name="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute">
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)">
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute"/> class.
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.#ctor(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.TestMethodAttribute)">
            Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute"/> class.
            It can also be called by a derived <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute"/> which will be called by a derived <see cref="T:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestClassAttribute"/>.
            <param name="testMethodAttribute">The wrapped test method.</param>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.UseSTASynchronizationContext">
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.ExecuteCoreAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ITestMethod)">
            <param name="testMethod">The test method to execute.</param>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.STATestMethodAttribute.ExecuteAsync(Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting.ITestMethod)">

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the changes in MSTest 4.1.0:

1. **`Execute` → `ExecuteAsync`**: The old `Execute(ITestMethod)` method returning `TestResult[]` has been replaced with `ExecuteAsync(ITestMethod)` which is virtual and presumably returns `Task<TestResult[]>`.

2. **`TestTimeout` removed**: The `TestTimeout` enum/class no longer exists. The `TimeoutAttribute` only takes an `int` (milliseconds). For infinite timeout, we should use `System.Threading.Timeout.Infinite` which is `-1`.

Let me check what `ExecuteAsync` returns.

data.content:
Build succeeds with 0 errors. Here's what changed:

**Two breaking changes in MSTest 4.1.0 and the fixes:**

1. **`Execute` → `ExecuteAsync`** — The synchronous `TestMethodAttribute.Execute(ITestMethod)` was removed. Override `ExecuteAsync` instead, which returns `Task<TestResult[]>`.

2. **`TestTimeout.Infinite` removed** — Replace with `System.Threading.Timeout.Infinite` (which is `-1`).

The remaining warnings are pre-existing (trivial `Assert.IsTrue(true)` placeholders and an optional caller-info suggestion).
